responses.question: In a typical week, what is the total number of hours you work across all your roles combined?
How many hours per week do you spend face-to-face (or on calls) with customers — across presales and delivery?
What are the main activities that make up your customer-facing time?
responses.answer: Not applicable to my roles. Don't have any direct customer interaction. Work with in region and internal stakeholders.

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Business Partner responsibilities?
In your Business Partner role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer:
Deal Reviews and planning
Pre-sales
Daily meetings and pre-sales strategy for enterprise deals where GDC >80%

responses.question: Still in your Business Partner role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer: I do not feel any activities are low-value or unproductive.

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Cloud Leader responsibilities?
In your Cloud Leader role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer: I have regular cadences with all my SPOCs to check progress and growth initiatives. Identify Areas of improvement and plan for growth beyond just delivery excellence.

responses.question: Still in your Cloud Leader role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer: I feel staffing should be handed off to Ops effectively

responses.question: What is the single biggest thing that prevents you from spending time on what matters most in your role?
responses.answer: I am able to manage all roles and tasks effectively. Meetings are prioritised well so I don't have any overhead there. Overall no challenges observed within the roles I am playing.

responses.question: If you could redesign your week, what would you spend significantly more time on — and what would you hand off or stop doing?
responses.answer: I would stop doing staffing, utilization reviews, handoffs , being the escalation point for every issue, reacting to every operational fire. I would spend more time on self learning and enablement.
